summary.intent: Build a fully interactive browser-in-browser viewing feature in the moltis UI, allowing users to create, view, and interact with browser sessions from the settings page.
summary.outcome: Delivered a working interactive browser viewer with session creation, live screencast via CDP, click/scroll relay, URL autocomplete, screenshot prefetching, and sandbox support — resolving multiple bugs along the way including null deserialization crashes, shared manager isolation, WebSocket subscription filtering, and coordinate mapping errors.

repo: The gateway has two separate subsystems (BrowserTool and RealBrowserService) that each created independent BrowserManager instances — sessions created by the AI tool were invisible to the UI's API until shared via Arc<OnceCell>

The WebSocket broadcast system uses an explicit subscription filter (broadcast.rs:100-102) — events are silently dropped if the client doesn't subscribe to that event type by name; 'browser.screencast.frame' was missing from websocket.js
CDP Page.startScreencast relay exits immediately when tx.send() has zero receivers — the relay task can die before any subscriber connects, causing a permanent 'no frames' state
validate_url() only allows http/https schemes — about:blank was rejected, silently failing new session creation
serde #[serde(default)] handles missing JSON keys but NOT explicit null values — LLMs like MiniMax send null for every optional field, causing u64 deserialization failures

The default viewport (2560x1440@2x) is optimized for agent screenshots but causes layout and coordinate issues in interactive viewing — websites render for the massive viewport, captchas appear off-center
CDP mouseWheel events require BOTH deltaX and deltaY to be present — conditional omission of deltaX when 0 causes CDP to reject the event entirely
Canvas event listeners attached via useEffect([]) never fire if the canvas is conditionally rendered — the effect runs once on mount before the canvas exists; ref callbacks are the correct pattern

code.finding: Added obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) to strip null values from LLM tool call payloads before serde deserialization
Subscription filter: if !client.is_subscribed_to(event) { continue } — silently drops events for clients not subscribed to that type
Original relay exited on SendError (zero receivers); fixed to continue loop and drop frames when no subscribers rather than terminating
mouseWheel CDP event now always sends both delta_x and delta_y; previously conditionally omitted deltaX when 0, causing CDP rejection
Default viewport changed from 2560x1440@2x to 1440x900@1x for interactive viewing sessions
Canvas coordinate mapping must use img.naturalWidth/naturalHeight (actual JPEG dimensions) and add offset_top from screencast metadata for click accuracy; offsetX/offsetY avoids border offset issues
Missing 'browser.screencast.frame' in the subscription list — added to ensure frames are not filtered out by broadcast.rs
useEffect([]) pattern fails for conditionally rendered canvas — replaced with ref callback (canvasRefCallback) that fires on DOM insertion/removal

workflow: Adding diagnostic tracing at every stage of a relay chain (CDP → registry → service → WebSocket) was essential to isolate which stage was dropping events
Integration tests with real browser sessions (marked #[ignore] for CI, runnable locally) caught the deltaX/deltaY CDP bug that unit tests would have missed
YubiKey-signed git pushes require manual interaction — background push tasks reliably fail without a tap; always retry explicitly after tap
Playwright e2e tests added alongside Rust integration tests provide dual coverage of the same feature at different layers
Screencast metadata (offset_top, device dimensions) from CDP is the authoritative source for coordinate mapping — not CSS layout or hardcoded values

friction: YubiKey tap required for every git push, causing repeated push failures in background tasks and requiring manual intervention multiple times

The 'Waiting for first frame' bug required four separate investigation rounds before the actual root cause (missing WebSocket subscription) was found — earlier fixes (relay keepalive, null stripping) were valid but not the root cause
Google suggestions CORS for autocomplete required using client=firefox endpoint workaround
Parallel Rust tests conflicted over browser profile lock files — requires --test-threads=1 for integration tests
Click offset debugging was complicated by multiple compounding issues: border offset, hardcoded aspect ratio, missing offset_top, and wrong scale source all contributing simultaneously
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko architecture with GStreamer + VP8/VP9) identified as the right long-term direction for <100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth improvement over CDP JPEG screencast
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast (eliminate base64 overhead, ~33% bandwidth reduction) — identified but not yet implemented
Per-agent browser profiles for session isolation — currently single global profile dir shared across all sessions
'Create Session from UI' feature fully working but per-agent profile isolation not built
Export Cookies button was removed from UI but backend cookie export/import via CDP remains available — may need re-exposing
Playwright e2e tests for canvas interaction (click accuracy, scroll) are written but rely on real browser availability — CI may skip them
